series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office (SAO)
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.
See GR.54018 for Audit Plan
records.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to
archival review and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited
feedback received by an agency from the
public concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of
GR.11048.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
leadership, supervisory, managerial,
executive, administrative, substantive,
and other high‐level correspondence in
any media, including electronic
communication (email), pertaining to the
formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the programs, services, or
projects of an agency and the
administrative regulations, policies, and
procedures that govern them.
retention_statement: 4 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
GR.11008 should be used only for
correspondence that is not
included in or directly related to
another series on the agency's
approved schedule. For example, a
memorandum that documents an
appropriations request must be
retained for the minimum
retention period prescribed by
GR.11004; a letter concerning an
audit for that prescribed by
GR.11002, etc. For executive
orders, see GR.11011.

series_description:
Non‐administrative incoming/outgoing
and internal correspondence, in any
media, including electronic
communication (email), pertaining to or
arising from the routine operations of the
policies, programs, services, or projects of
an agency.
retention_statement: 2 years
comments:
See remarks for GR.11007. For
directives, see GR.11010.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, agency heads, and board
or commission members require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have
met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period.
SEE Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard
to pending litigation, or opinions
rendered for Public Information Act
Requests. See GR.11048 and
GR.11020. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Opinions and advice that set legal
precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. SEE Archival
Codes Special Note in the Texas
RRS 5th edition for more
information.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code), including records
provided to the legislature under section
552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the
structure of an agency and the
relationships and relative ranks of its
departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail
are not considered archival. VITAL.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
neither included in nor directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from
customers, clients, or stakeholders of an
agency, and the statistical data
maintained rating an agency's
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
For summary reports compiled
from customer surveys, see
GR.11067.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non‐personnel
individuals it regulates or serves, including
but not limited to training manuals,
course registration, class rosters, sign‐in
sheets, syllabuses, course outlines, and
similar training aids used in external
training programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
See GR.33030 for internal
personnel training materials. If the
training is grant‐funded, see
GR.47008. CAUTION: Does not
include hazardous material training
records (GR.54007).

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. SEE Archival
Codes Special Note in the Texas
RRS 5th edition for more
information.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed
with the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self‐evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description:
Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an
ongoing records series. Transitory records
are not essential to the fulfillment of
statutory obligations or to the
documentation of agency functions. SEE
Explanation of Terms in the Texas RRS 5th
edition for more information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should make certain
records are not part of another
records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part
of a records series that documents
the fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
destruction signoffs (EX.12001) or
in records disposition logs
(EX.12010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing
disposal of these records as part of
its records management plan
(EX.12014).

series_title:
Agendas and Minutes of
Open Meetings
series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils
that conduct open meetings as required
by Chapter 551, Government Code.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement will be met by
sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. CAUTION: This
records series and GR.11059,
GR.11060, GR.11061, and
GR.11062 must be used for those
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils, which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency. VITAL.

series_title:
Agendas and Minutes or
Audiovisual Recordings of
Closed Meetings
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: See caution comment at GR.11058.

series_description:
Audiovisual recordings of open meetings
of state boards, commissions,
committees, and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments:
CAUTION: It is an exception to the
AC+90 days retention that if an
agency does not prepare written
minutes, it must retain recordings
of open meetings permanently,
following the same retention
requirements of GR.11058. See
also caution comment at
GR.11058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments: See caution comment at GR.11058.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils, including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, public
comment forms, etc. Includes documents
sent in advance of meetings for briefing
purposes, some of which may not be
submitted at an actual meeting.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: See caution comment at GR.11058.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.
retention_statement: 1 year

series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
See GR.11068 for reports on
agency performance measures.
CAUTION: The FE + 3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the
records series is needed for
documentation of agency
performance measures. VITAL.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non‐
Fiscal) ‐ Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing
non‐fiscal reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See GR.11067 for reports produced
from raw data. CAUTION: Does not
include source documentation
used for information or data
included in or directly related to
another records series in this
schedule. For agency performance
measures documentation, see
GR.11064.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Annual, sub‐annual, or special reports or
studies on non‐fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by
advisory committees, or by consultants
under contract with an agency that are
not noted elsewhere in this schedule.
Includes reports distributed either
internally or to other entities.
retention_statement: 3 years
comments:
See GR.11065 for raw data used to
produce reports. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Reports are archival when they
deal with significant aspects of the
agency's programs. See Archival
Codes Special Note in the Texas
RRS 5th edition for more
information.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
See GR.11064 for documentation
used to produce reports on agency
performance measures

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining
to workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
retention_statement: 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures,
see GR.11064.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
rules, policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per
Government Code, 552.010.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self‐study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission's draft
report and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations. The final
version of the agency self‐
evaluation report must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_description:
Records that document the release of
records or information through any
method other than a Public Information
Act request or subpoena (including
employment verification).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See GR.11020 for records released
under the Public Information Act.
See GR.11076 for records produced
for a subpoena. See GR.11048 for
records produced for litigation.
CAUTION: Some records releases
may require longer retention
period. Agencies must determine if
longer retention period is required
based on any federal or state
statutes or regulations that apply
to the agency's functions. Agency
legal staff should be consulted.

series_description:
Waivers of liability, including statements
signed by volunteers acknowledging non‐
entitlement to benefits, agreeing to abide
by state agency policies, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If an accident occurs to
any person covered by a signed
waiver of liability, it must be
retained for the same period as
accident reports. See GR.54001 and
AD.54014.

series_description:
Copyright records which pertain to
employees’ original work. May include but
not limited to a copy of the work itself as
submitted to the United States Copyright
Office and the corresponding copyright
application, registration notice, and
supplementary documents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records that document selection criteria
and the history of awards both
administered by the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (TSLAC) or
applied for on behalf of TSLAC. Including,
but not limited to the following: eligibility
terms and selection criteria, award history
and information on funding sources,
award notifications, summary lists of
winners, biographies on winners, and
press releases.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Series includes records fitting to
URRS 11.1.003. See AD.1E055 for
Employee of the Quarter (EOQ)
Awards. See GR.11104 for award
selection records.

series_description:
Records that document the process of
selecting award winners of awards both
administered by the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (TSLAC) and
applied for on behalf of TSLAC. Including,
but not limited to the following:
applications, nomination letters,
recommendations, letters of award
notification or denial, letters accepting or
declining awards, demonstration of need
documentation, and any other related
documentation and correspondence.
retention_statement: 2 years
comments:
Series includes records fitting to
URRS 11.1.004. See AD.1E055 for
Employee of the Quarter (EOQ)
Awards. See GR.11103 for award
administration and history records.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design or
design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must
carry the same retention period
and archival code of the records
they support. VITAL.

series_description:
One copy of each state publication as
defined on page xi of the 5th Edition of
the Texas State Records Retention
Schedule (RRS), except a publication that
is subject to a different retention period in
this schedule. For example, agendas of
open meetings (GR.11058) also meet the
definition, but must be retained
permanently; Annual Financial Reports
(AD.1B002), Strategic Plans (GR.11055),
Legislative Appropriation Requests
(GR.11004), Annual or Biennial Narrative
Reports (GR.11066), and Reports on
Performance Measures (GR.11068), which
also meet the definition, are closely
associated with the appropriations
process and must be retained AC + 6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code, 441.103‐
441.105). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if
the publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
internet, consult the administrative
rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of
the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (13 TAC §§3.1‐
3.8).

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, and the commission's publication
development files. This includes all work
performed both inside and outside the
agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See GR.13001 for final State
Publications created from
development files. ARCHIVES
NOTE: Successive and substantive
drafts of major publications may be
archival. Major is defined by both
the publication's authorship and its
impact on Texas and Texans.
Original artwork, including photo
prints and negatives that have
significant value as evidence of
agency programs as well as the
potential for re‐use, may be
archival.

series_description:
Relatively long‐lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets
of complete and accurate electronic
records and associated processing files.
Examples include, but are not limited to,
data tables of relational databases used
by applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of
AC to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_title:
Software Programs and
Job Control Language
series_description:
Agency‐developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include
but are not limited to: user guides, system
or sub‐system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program
maintenance logs, change notices, data
element dictionaries, file layouts, code
books or tables, and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates
to application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Hardware and software components for
monitoring agency’s computer system
resources and performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
The disposal of monitoring records
that are automatically overwritten
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations
(EX.12001) or in records disposition
logs (EX.12010), but procedures
governing disposal of these records
are part of the records
management plan (EX.12014).

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems
development, data retention and
disposition, data ownership, production
control, system back‐up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
See GR.21009. VITAL.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to enter and
reconcile data sets submitted for
processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Information verifying the quality of
system, hardware, or software operations.
Records include but are not limited to:
records of errors or failures and the loss
of data resulting from such failures;
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error‐free processing; checks of
changes put into production; transaction
histories; and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
For quality control records related
to non‐IT procedures, see
GR.52018.

series_description:
A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user‐specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state‐
owned computer.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
The disposal of internet history
records need not be documented
through destruction authorizations
(EX.12001) or in records disposition
logs (EX.12010), but procedures
governing disposal of these records
are part of the records
management plan (EX.12014).

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_title:
Community Service and
Volunteer Program Files
series_description:
Includes applications, agreements,
waivers, registration, correspondence,
forms signed by docents or volunteers,
parental approval acknowledgements,
training documentation, evaluations, time
and attendance records, or any other
applicable record.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Series based on 3.1.002 in RRS.
CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work‐
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments: CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and
are imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance. Personnel
Corrective Action Documentation is also
referred to as an Improvement Plan, but is
not a disciplinary action.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under GR.31021,
all documentation from this series
used to support disciplinary action
must be retained for the minimum
retention period prescribed by
GR.31021. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to
the best interests of the state, the agency,
or the employee work force; and for
failure to improve performance or
conduct following imposition of corrective
action. May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation. May also be used to
document evidence of employee self‐
improvement efforts, as well as favorable
and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion
or transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different master test (different in
terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should
be retained for the period
indicated.

series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills
test papers and checklists of job
applicants or of current personnel taking a
test to qualify for promotion or transfer.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Records documenting the planning,
development, implementation,
administration and evaluation of in‐house
training programs, including but not
limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign‐in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in in‐house training
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
See GR.11043 for external training
records. See AD.1E048 for
individual employee training
records. CAUTION: Does not
include hazardous material training
records (GR.54007).

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick
Leave Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: For requests requiring
approval by Human Resources, see
AD.1E046.

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: See GR.47008 for
retention of grant‐funded records.
Retention requirements may vary
depending on the specific funding
agency.

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: See GR.47008 for
retention of grant‐funded records.
Retention requirements may vary
depending on the specific funding
agency.

series_description:
Internal and external periodic fiscal
management reports and associated
worksheets, including federal financial
reports, salary reports, HUB reports,
investment performance reports, etc.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: This series does not
include fiscal reports created to
fulfill grant requirements. See
GR.47008. VITAL.

series_description:
This series documents state, federal, and
other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by state
agencies. It includes grant authorization
records, which provide evidence of the
award of grants to or by agencies;
grant/project financial or performance
reports, which are periodic reports of
financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements
may vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Records in this
series may be subject to
Government Code 441.1855.
Agencies must ensure that records
are retained for the appropriate
retention period. VITAL.

series_description:
Non‐awarded, denied, or unfunded grant
applications and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit
slips, debit and credit notices,
reconciliations, and other banking related
records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers
and revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities,
and construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Series includes records fitting to
5.1.001a and 5.1.001b in the RRS.
For Bid Documentation see
GR.53007. For Building
Construction Contract and
Inspection Records see GR.52028.
VITAL.

series_description:
Records documenting incoming or
outgoing deliveries, including through
private courier services.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
Requisitions for In‐Agency
or Inter‐Agency Copying
and Printing Services
series_description:
Includes word processing and data
processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Licenses and permits obtained from
external agencies or organizations in order
to perform operations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
licenses and permits issued by an
agency as part of its statutory
responsibilities.

series_title:
Photocopier and Fax
Usage Logs & Reports
series_description:
Registers or logs of print copies and fax
transmissions made by user or in total.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other
agencies, including any documentation
used to determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life,
automobile, and other policies for
government property and personnel
including supporting documentation
relevant to the implementation,
modification, renewal, or replacement of
policies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Series includes records fitting to
5.1013a and 5.1013a in the RRS.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that
establish standard office procedures for
an agency. For example, agency style
manuals, telephone protocols, mail room
procedures, print shop and photocopy
ordering instructions, purchasing
procedures, cataloging procedures, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Surveillance videos of buildings, facilities,
vehicles, or other state property.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
If video is needed for an accident
investigation, see GR.54001 or
AD.54014. If the video is needed as
evidence in litigation, see
GR.11048. The disposal of
surveillance videos need not be
documented through destruction
signoffs or in records disposition
logs.

series_description:
Project records related to planning,
design, construction, conversion, or
modernization of state facilities,
structures, infrastructure, and systems,
including feasibility, screening, and
implementation studies; topographical
and soil surveys and reports; as‐builts;
laboratory test reports; environmental
impact statements; correspondence;
successful and unsuccessful bid
documentation and other related
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
See GR.52003 and GR.52028 for
further retention of completed
building documentation. See
AD.1G017 for additional bid
documentation retention periods,
including AD.1G018 for invalid bids
that do not meet agency
submission requirements.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Archival review
designation is for state‐owned
buildings only. VITAL.

series_title:
Building Plans and
Specifications ‐ State
Owned
series_description:
Includes architectural and engineering
drawings, profiles, and blueprints of
planning, design, construction,
conversion, or modernization of state‐
owned facilities, structures, infrastructure,
and systems.
retention_statement: Life of Asset
comments:
See GR.52002 and GR.52028.
VITAL.

series_description:
Records documenting the inventorying,
maintenance, usage, checkout, and
disposal of supplies, equipment, and
property of a state agency. Includes lost,
stolen, and damage reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
See GR.52008 for the maintenance
logs of individual pieces of
equipment.

series_title:
Inspection, Repair, and
Maintenance Records –
Equipment
series_description:
Equipment history file, which may include
logbooks and requests for installation,
moves, service, repair, etc.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years
comments:
For service agreements or
contracts related to equipment
repairs and service, retain in
accordance with GR.51001. For
vehicle maintenance records, see
GR.56003.

series_description:
Equipment manuals, owner’s manuals,
instructional manuals, or other similar
documentation for government owned
equipment, except for equipment manuals
noted elsewhere in this schedule.
retention_statement: Life of Asset

series_description:
Warranties providing that the equipment
will perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Listing shows all additions, changes,
deletions, and transfer times for the
monthly processing period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See GR.52006 for annual inventory
listing.

series_description:
Documentation and reporting on
adherence to procedures that ensure
quality of a process, product, or service.
retention_statement: 2 years
comments:
See GR.22013 for quality control
records related to IT procedures.

series_description:
Requests or work orders for repairs or
maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or
equipment completed by internal agency
personnel or external service providers.
retention_statement: 1 year

series_description:
Reservation logs or similar records
relating to the use of agency facilities such
as meeting rooms, auditoriums, etc.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Reports summarizing efficiency of facility
space utilization, which may include data
on room usage, demand, allocation, and
capacity.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Building Construction
Contract and Inspection
Records
series_description:
Building construction contracts, surety
bonds, and inspection records.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 10 years
comments: See also GR.52002 and GR.52003.

series_description:
Includes copies of requests for binding
from the agency divisions and the binding
slips sent to vendor with the books, and
packing slips showing what was returned.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Occupational Accident
Reports and Associated
Documentation
series_description:
Accident or occupational disease reports
(by supervisors and employees) and other
associated reports required to be
submitted to the State Office of Risk
Management (SORM) or its predecessors
or maintained internally on accident
frequency.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 5 years
comments:
See AD.54014 for non‐employee
accidents.

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of an agency facilities
in cases of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Fire, safety, and emergency drill, alarm,
and other inspection records of agency
facilities and equipment, including orders
issued by inspectors to correct
deficiencies in compliance with any code
or regulations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: For inspection reports of
building construction, see
GR.52028.

series_description:
Records of training given to employees in
an agency hazard communications
program. May include but is not limited to
date of class, roster of attendees, subjects
covered, and instructors.
retention_statement: 5 years
comments:
See AD.1E048 for individual
employee training records.

series_description:
Plan that provides information to
employees about the hazardous chemicals
to which they may be exposed to in their
workplace.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 5 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
List of each hazardous chemical normally
present in the workplace.
retention_statement: 30 years

series_description:
Safety Data Sheets (SDS) that list
information relating to occupational
safety and health for the use of various
substances and products.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: If Workplace Chemical
Lists (GR.54009) are not
maintained, these records must be
maintained for 30 years.

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description:
Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated
systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: VITAL. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code,
412.054) to mitigate the damage of
potential events that could endanger an
agency’s ability to function. Includes
disaster preparedness, response, and
recovery plans.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments: VITAL. CONFIDENTIAL.
legal_citation:
Labor Code §412.054
TAC, Title 1, Part 10,
Chapter 202,
Subchapter B, Rule
§202.24.

series_description:
Includes working papers and agency risk
assessment used to develop the plan, per
Texas Internal Auditing Act requirement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
See GR.11002 for individual Audit
records not related to the
development of the Audit Plan.

series_description:
Telephone logs created for internal
documentation purposes, station activity
reports of internal listings of
incoming/outgoing telephone activity to
individual telephone stations, operator
call transfers, summary detail reports, and
system activity reports of internal listings
of all incoming/outgoing agency
telephone activity.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments: See GR.41001 for telephone bills.

series_title:
Vehicle Inspection, Repair,
and Maintenance Records
series_description:
Records and documentation related to
inspections, repairs, and maintenance for
state vehicles.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 1 year
comments:
See GR.52008 for nonvehicle
equipment maintenance records.

series_description:
Records documenting issuance of parking
permits and assignments.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
See GR.41009 for payment of
permit fees.

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act (100 Day Report).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.
CAUTION: If an agency does not
produce a biennial or annual
narrative report as described in
GR.11066, then the archival
requirement is met by sending the
required copies of this annual
financial report to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission.

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description:
Vehicle titles, registration information,
and owner manuals for state vehicles.
retention_statement: Life of Asset
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records used to document the
registration of vendors to conduct
business with the State of Texas and the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission (TSLAC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests. See
AD.31042.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity)
reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: For criminal history
checks, see AD.31026. For records
of preemployments skills tests; see
GR.33027 and GR.33028.

series_description:
Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances or complaints
against personnel policies, working
conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U S
Department of Labor (DOL). See
GR.11048.

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee, including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination
of employment.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify
employment. Includes: name; social
security number; exact dates of
employment; last known address; and
most recent public access authorization.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
comments:
For public access option forms, see
AD.1E056. VITAL. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed
listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position
vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and
seasonality of employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce
Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I‐9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life, health,
vision, dental, disability, and other types
of insurance or benefits offered by the
State of Texas to its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: For documents that
serve as payroll deduction
authorizations, see AD.1E057.

series_description:
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc. Includes
letters of recommendation and support,
applications, and other correspondence
and notes that document the screening
and selection process needed for the
Employee of the Quarter (EOQ) program
committee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security
number, and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code 552.024.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must
be kept as long as the former
employee verification records. See
AD.1E035. VITAL.

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court‐ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments: CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for regular
employees, contractors, subcontractors,
and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and conditions of
employment of an individual and a
contract with a vendor for temporary
staffing services.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption,
passengers carried, and other related
operational information.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of
agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received,
and similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation/evaluations
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.)
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the
ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets,
and payroll history.
retention_statement: 4 years

series_description:
Records defining the wage or salary rate
for each position in the agency expressed
in dollars, grades, or step numbers.
retention_statement: 2 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work‐related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or services by
direct purchase or bid.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See GR.11020 and GR.11021 for
public information requests.
CAUTION: If the request for
information leads to request for
proposal or bid, the request for
information documentation should
be retained in accordance with
AD.1G017 or AD.1G018, as
applicable.

series_description:
W‐9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer
identification number (TIN) for reporting
information to the Internal Revenue
Service (IRS). This includes W‐9 forms
received by a state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Reports of accidents to adults and minors
on state property or in any other situation
in which the state agency could be a party
to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Includes accession logs, deeds of gift, loan
documentation, location information,
replevin files, legislators’ Texas Govt Code
Section 306 and 323 forms, and record
copies of memorandums of
understanding.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: VITAL. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Accession files, appraisal files, shelf lists,
inventories, Capitol Art files, flag files, and
conservation/preservation evaluations
and condition reports. Data related to
conservation/preservation treatment files.
Includes Regional Historical Resource
Depository (RHRD) donor files, agreement
records, and collection history records.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Finding aids and similar records for
managing the TSLAC archival holdings.
Includes preservation surveys, finding aid
processing files, processor and appraiser
notes, digitization management
documentation,
conservation/preservation treatment files,
and copies of Request for Authority to
Dispose of State Records (RMD 102).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Original RMD 102 forms
for all state agencies are retained
by the SLRM Division. For the
agency's official record copy of
RMD 102, see EX.12001.

series_title:
Patron Registrations and
Archival Materials Usage
Reports
series_description:
Patron Registrations: registration and
agreement cards signed by patrons
registering to use the library services and
archival collections Archival Material
Usage Reports: information related to
materials requested, pulled, and used by
patrons Includes record group series and
dates of requests.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
User registration cards and SIRSI user
account.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years
comments:
User accounts that are renewed
are considered active user
accounts. Inactive or expired user
accounts will follow FE retention
period. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Forms track routing and use of archival
holdings.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years

series_description:
Information related to materials
requested, pulled, and used by patrons
Includes record group series and dates of
requests.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 20 years

series_title:
Procedures Manuals for
the Treatment of Agency
Holdings
series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Correspondence and other
documentation with potential transfers or
donations of historical materials
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Correspondence,
notes, etc, with donors who
transfer manuscript collections to
the ARIS Division of TSLAC will be
added to the manuscript accession
files. See AIN 3A001a. VITAL.
CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Historical documentation of exhibits,
publications, and events.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
Capital Asset Records ‐
Sam Houston Center
series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost,
depreciation schedules or summaries used
for capital outlay budgeting, and property
sale, auction, or disposal records of
agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records granting/denying permission to
reproduce images of items in the
collection. Includes permission forms and
correspondence requesting permission to
publish images from archival collections.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Memos and check in/out sheets to
vendors since 1977. Includes information
or image numbers, description, and work
performed.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Research, pricing, and documentation
research and other preliminary
documentation needed for the
procurement of grant funded goods and
services for the Conservation Lab.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Loan requests from Interlibrary Loan (ILL)
and Regional Historical Resource
Depository (RHRD).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_title:
Regional Historical
Resource Depository
(RHRD) Microfilm Projects
series_description:
RHRD microfilm holdings include packing
lists, camera operator reports,
inventories, finding guides, and
correspondence with Genealogical Society
of Utah and RHRDs.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Materials withdrawn by federal depository
libraries in Texas.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Depository Library Program (FDLP) FDLP
plans, inspections, lost/missing
documents list, finding aids, and
Bernan/Folland/Marcive shipping list
database.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Information for offsite storage requests of
archival material and when materials were
returned for offsite storage. Includes
dates and box numbers of when the
materials were requested and returned to
offsite storage.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Do not confuse this
series with State Records Center
Transmittal Requests (SL.5E019) or
agency records transmittal forms
(EX.12006).

series_title:
Donation of Library
Materials and Monetary
Contributions Records
series_description:
Donation forms filled out and submitted
by individuals or groups donating
monetary contributions and published
materials to the ownership of the Texas
State Library and Archives Commission
(TSLAC). Includes donation form, all
correspondence, thank you letters, copies
of checks and monetary donation
information, and any other pertinent
information regarding donation
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 7 years
comments:
CAUTION: Do not confuse this
series with records that document
the donation and accession of
archival, historical materials, or
permanent endowments See
AR.3A001a, AR.3A027, and
AR.3B008.

series_title:
Texas State Publications
Depository Program
Publications List Form
series_description:
Publications List Form, any variation of
this form, or any correspondence that
accompanies a Texas State Publications
Depository Program publication
submission.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_title:
Library Collection and
Information Services
Program Documentation
series_description:
Documents that describe or clarify history
or significant changes in library collection
areas including but not limited to the US
Documents, Texas Documents, and Dewey
collections. Includes genealogy, reference,
and archives brochures, newspaper
clippings, memos, letters, discussions on
the changes to hours of operation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation of software upgrades,
contract renewals, client care cases
opened and completed, and history of our
partner library participation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing
nonfiscal reports. Spreadsheet used to
report historical collection statistics.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. See
GR.11064 for Agency Performance
Measures Documentation. See
GR.11067 for for reports produced
from raw data.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, and
audiovisual records.
retention_statement: 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Specific social
media accounts representing the
agency may require archival
review. Contact the State Archives
when these records have met their
retention periods.

series_description:
Documents authorizing final disposition of
records under a certified records retention
schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction
authorizations are maintained as
part of the records disposition logs
(GR.12010), then a longer retention
period applies.

series_description:
A records retention schedule (ie form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Official record copies of all
approved state agency retention
schedules (see SL.50004) are
retained for 50 years by the State
and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Do not confuse this
series with State Records Center
Transmittal Requests (SL.5E019) or
Archives Retrieval and Return Log
(AR.3C050).

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by the agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of
a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_title:
Records and Information
Management Policies and
Procedures
series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Documents that show the legal
establishment of libraries.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Paper application form signed by library
officials seeking Library System
membership.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
Spreadsheets and/or databases of
summary statistical data of annual
quantitative performance also used for
historical reports.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing correspondence
to/from entities seeking changes in their
Public Library Annual Report
documentation.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
All documentation relating to the Schools
and Libraries Program of the Universal
Service Fund, which is administered by the
Universal Service Administrative Company
(USAC) under the direction of the Federal
Communications Commission (FCC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
See: Federal Communication
Commission Report (FCC 04‐190).
VITAL.

series_description:
A database that lists contact information
for Libraries in Texas.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_title:
State Agency Records
Program Administration
Correspondence
series_description:
Forms or correspondence filed with TSLAC
by state agency heads declaring to act as
or designate an individual as the agency's
Records Management Officer. Includes
directives impacting the records
management program.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
State Agency Retention
Schedule Review Files ‐
Working Copies
series_description:
Working copies of retention schedules
submitted for certification or
amendments.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Only the working copy submitted
for final approval must retained in
the R02 files. CAUTION: Be careful
not to dispose of other review
working files. See SL.50003.

series_title:
State Agency Retention
Schedule Review Working
Files (R02)
series_description:
Documentation of the retention schedule
review process, including checklists,
notes, analyst review memoranda and
correspondence. Working file may include
storage or archival reports, as applicable
to the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
After approval of recertification or
amendment, transfer the State
Agency Retention Schedule and
Amendments (STARS) checklist to
the R04 file (SL.50005) for further
retention.

series_title:
State Agency Retention
Schedule Certification and
Amendment Files (R01)
series_description:
SLRM's official record copy of all state
agencies records retention schedules,
including any amendments with TSLAC
acting as the state's agent. Includes the
current approved retention schedule
(Form SLR 105), any amendments (Form
SLR 122), any signature approval pages
(Form SLR 105C), and all recertification
correspondence with the state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
See EX.12005 for the official record
copy of TSLAC's approved records
retention schedule.

series_title:
State Agency Approved
Records Retention
Schedules (R04)
series_description:
Includes the superseded retention
schedule, all amendments, signature
approval pages, State Agency Retention
Schedule and Amendments (STARS)
checklist transferred from SL.50003, and
all administrative correspondence to the
state agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 50 years
comments:
The official record copy of all state
agency's superseded retention
schedules, including any
amendments. See EX.12005 for the
official record copy of TSLAC's
approved records retention
schedule. VITAL.

series_description:
A consolidated consulting log used for
performance measure reporting.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: The FE + 3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the
records series is needed for
documentation of agency
performance measures For
individual Consulting Files, see
SL.5C013.

series_title:
Request for Authority to
Dispose of State Records
series_description:
Documentation (Form RMD 102) used to
obtain approval to destroy all official state
records not listed on an agency's
approved records retention schedule.
Includes destruction requests,
acknowledgements, and supporting
documentation from state agencies, as
well as a copy of any relevant reports.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 20 years
comments:
For documentation of premature
loss or destruction of local
government records, see SL.50009.
For TSLAC's agency destruction
authorizations, see EX.12001.
VITAL.

series_title:
Documentation of
Premature Loss or
Destruction of Local
Government Records
series_description:
Forms or memorandum sent to TSLAC by
local governments documenting the
premature loss or destruction of local
government records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 20 years
comments:
Form SLR 501 is now obsolete, but
documentation may still be
received through correspondence.

series_description:
Includes all forms filed and records
control schedules, records management
ordinances/policies, and any
administrative correspondence with local
government entities.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments:
Hardcopies of compliance records
are the document of record,
electronic files are convenience
copies. VITAL.

series_description:
Petitions from Countywide
superintendent of the County Clerk's
office to retain school records
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Requests from state agencies for transfer
of files or boxes to or from the State
Records Center facility.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Do not confuse this
series with agency records
transmittal forms (EX.12006) or
Archives Retrieval and Return Log
(AR.3C050).

series_description:
Working papers that document records
destruction services of the State Records
Center (SRC).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
All disposition index information was
migrated to Excel spreadsheets in April
2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 50 years
comments:
All disposition index information
was migrated to Excel spreadsheets
in April 2015.

series_description:
Documentation of daily delivery routes
may include work, duty, shift, crew, or
case schedules, rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Authorizations and other records used to
provide access to disaster recovery vault.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Forms and index documenting the transfer
of records to storage or a transfer of legal
custody. Includes both the State Records
Center (SRC) and imaging files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments: VITAL.

series_description:
Paper copies of indexes regarding
permanent removals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments: See SL.5E009 for perm‐outs. VITAL.

series_description:
Used to verify invoiced activities with
physical signature on report. SRC keeps
this copy, customer receives a copy with
driver's signature.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years
comments:
Perm‐outs are handled through
Texlinx and are reflected in the
item's status after May 2015.

series_description:
Items rejected for storage for various
reasons prior to Texlinx.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Replaced with Texlinx Delivery
Report.

series_description:
Information about the studio recording
and production of books on tape, such as
who narrated, monitored, and reviewed
the book.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Includes patron applications that are
active, on hold, transferred out or
suspended.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: VITAL. CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Forms submitted by schools to register
eligible students for service from TBP.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments: CONFIDENTIAL.

series_description:
Database that tracks reference questions
and answers for Disability Information and
Referral Center (DIRC) Coordinator.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 5 years

series_title:
Interlibrary Loan (ILL) and
Multistate Center Request
Forms
series_description: Forms requesting ILL titles.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
